This stage is the transport bridge between the public RMCP client API and the concrete ways MCP traffic actually moves at runtime. It sits in cross-cutting infrastructure used whenever a client session is created or reconnected, translating higher-level MCP operations onto process stdio, in-process transports, or HTTP.

At the top, rmcp-client/src/lib.rs is the facade consumers import: it exposes the client, transport abstractions, OAuth helpers, and selected protocol types while wiring the internal modules together. For non-networked or embedded servers, in_process_transport.rs defines how to create a fresh in-process transport stream on demand so reconnect logic does not depend on server launch details. For external tools, executor_process_transport.rs runs MCP over an executor-managed child process, converting JSON-RPC messages to newline-delimited stdio, tolerating lagged event delivery, and separating stderr logging.

HTTP-backed MCP is assembled from several cooperating pieces. reqwest_http_client.rs performs real outbound HTTP requests, while rpc_http_client.rs forwards those requests over the exec-server RPC channel and rebuilds streamed responses remotely. http_client_adapter.rs then maps that shared HttpClient capability onto RMCP’s streamable HTTP interface, including SSE and JSON-RPC response handling. www_authenticate.rs extracts Bearer insufficient-scope details from 403 responses, and streamable_http_retry.rs retries transient initialization and request failures under a shared deadline.
